questions.q: Which deity is the divine owner of the Pashupatastra?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is the personal astra of Shiva, wielded in his aspect as Pashupati, the Lord of Creatures.

questions.q: The name 'Pashupatastra' is derived from which title of Shiva?
questions.explanation: The weapon takes its name from Pashupati, meaning 'Lord of Creatures', one of Shiva's well-known epithets.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, which Pandava prince receives the Pashupatastra from Shiva?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Shiva grants the Pashupatastra to Arjuna as a supreme boon.

questions.q: In which great epic does Arjuna obtain the Pashupatastra from Shiva?
questions.explanation: Arjuna's acquisition of the Pashupatastra is told in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: The Pashupatastra is best described as which kind of object?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is a celestial weapon, or astra, regarded as the most fearsome among them.

questions.q: In the episode where Shiva grants the Pashupatastra to Arjuna, what disguise does Shiva take?
questions.explanation: In the Kiratarjuniya episode, Shiva appears in the guise of a Kirata, a mountain hunter, to test Arjuna.

questions.q: According to its lore, against what kind of opponent must the Pashupatastra never be used?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is so destructive that it is never to be used against a lesser foe, lest it bring needless devastation.

questions.q: Which weapon is most closely associated with Shiva, the lord of the Pashupatastra?
questions.explanation: Shiva is iconically depicted bearing the trishula, or trident, his characteristic weapon.

questions.q: What is the literal meaning of 'Pashupati', the title from which the Pashupatastra is named?
questions.explanation: 'Pashupati' translates as 'Lord of Creatures' or 'Lord of Beasts', signifying Shiva as protector of all living beings.

questions.q: The Kiratarjuniya episode, in which the Pashupatastra is granted, occurs during which part of the Pandavas' lives?
questions.explanation: The Kiratarjuniya episode takes place during the Pandavas' forest exile, when Arjuna undertakes austerities to gain divine weapons.

questions.q: Before granting the Pashupatastra, Shiva tests Arjuna in a dispute over which hunted animal?
options: A boar (in the form of a demon)
questions.explanation: In the Kiratarjuniya, Arjuna and the disguised Shiva both claim to have struck a boar, which was the demon Muka in disguise.

questions.q: The classical Sanskrit poem 'Kiratarjuniya', which narrates the granting of the Pashupatastra, was composed by which poet?
questions.explanation: The 'Kiratarjuniya' is a celebrated Sanskrit mahakavya composed by the poet Bharavi.

questions.q: Which austerity did Arjuna chiefly perform to win Shiva's favour and the Pashupatastra?
options: Severe penance and meditation in the forest
A pilgrimage to all sacred rivers
questions.explanation: Arjuna undertook severe tapasya, intense penance and meditation, in the Himalayas to obtain the weapon from Shiva.

questions.q: According to its lore, the Pashupatastra is capable of which ultimate feat?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is described as capable of destroying creation itself, making it the most fearsome of all astras.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which mount (vahana) is associated with Shiva, the lord of the Pashupatastra?
questions.explanation: Shiva's vahana is the bull Nandi, his devoted mount and gatekeeper.

questions.q: Who is traditionally named as the consort of Shiva, the divine wielder of the Pashupatastra?
questions.explanation: Parvati, the daughter of the mountain king Himavan, is the consort of Shiva.

questions.q: According to tradition, by what means could the Pashupatastra be invoked?
options: Only by writing a sacred mantra
By thought, word, look, or bow
questions.explanation: The Mahabharata describes that the Pashupatastra could be discharged by mind, word, eye, or bow, requiring great mastery to wield.

questions.q: In the Ramayana tradition, the Pashupatastra is held to belong to which deity?
questions.explanation: Across the texts the Pashupatastra is consistently the weapon of Shiva, the supreme destroyer.

questions.q: Which other supremely destructive astra is the Pashupatastra most often compared with in the epics?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is frequently compared to the Brahmastra, both regarded as among the most destructive celestial weapons.

questions.q: In the Kiratarjuniya episode, what was Arjuna's attitude that pleased Shiva even while they fought?
questions.explanation: Shiva was pleased by Arjuna's undaunted courage and devotion during their combat, and so revealed himself and granted the boon.

questions.q: The Pashupatastra is classified in the Indian mythological card deck under which type?
questions.explanation: As a celestial weapon, the Pashupatastra is classified as an artifact, a sacred object of power.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, why did Arjuna seek divine weapons such as the Pashupatastra during the exile?
options: To prepare for the coming war against the Kauravas
questions.explanation: Arjuna sought celestial weapons to prepare the Pandavas for the eventual war against the Kauravas at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: In the Kiratarjuniya episode, the boar that Arjuna and the disguised Shiva both shoot was actually which demon in disguise?
questions.explanation: The boar was the asura Muka, sent to slay Arjuna, whom both Arjuna and the Kirata pierced with their arrows.

questions.q: After winning the Pashupatastra, Arjuna ascends to whose celestial abode to receive further divine weapons?
questions.explanation: Following the Kirata episode, Arjuna travels to the heaven of Indra, his divine father, to obtain additional celestial arms.

questions.q: Which name is also used for Shiva's bow, distinct from the Pashupatastra weapon itself?
questions.explanation: Shiva's celebrated bow is named Pinaka, which is distinct from the Pashupatastra missile-weapon he bestows.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the Pashupatastra is said to be guarded so that it may not be used carelessly, for fear of what consequence?
options: The loss of the user's voice
The destruction of the entire world
questions.explanation: Because the Pashupatastra could annihilate the whole world if loosed without cause, it was never to be discharged against a weak adversary.

questions.q: According to the Mahabharata, Shiva is also known as Mahadeva when bestowing the Pashupatastra; what does 'Mahadeva' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Mahadeva' means 'the Great God', a foremost epithet of Shiva, the giver of the Pashupatastra.

questions.q: Which mountainous region forms the setting where Arjuna performs penance and obtains the Pashupatastra?
questions.explanation: Arjuna performed his austerities in the Himalayas, where Shiva appeared to him as the Kirata.

questions.q: In the broader epic tradition, the Pashupatastra is counted among weapons capable of countering or surpassing which other supreme astra?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra is held to be supreme among astras, able to overcome even the Brahmastra in the epic accounts.

questions.q: Within the Shaiva tradition, 'Pashupati' as a divine title presents Shiva chiefly in which role toward all living beings?
options: As their judge of the dead
questions.explanation: As Pashupati, Shiva is revered as the lord and protector of all creatures, the source from which the weapon's name derives.
